nodes.label: Data Science Exam Bible (21CSS303T)
Course 21CSS303T — Data Science, SRM Institute
Exam Structure: 3 hours / 100 marks
Part A — MCQ (20 marks, 15 questions, answer 10 best)
Part B — Short Answer (40 marks, 5 questions, answer 4)
Part C — Long Answer (40 marks, 3 questions, answer 2)
Time Allocation: Part A 27min / Part B 72min / Part C 72min / Buffer 9min
Section 1 — Intelligence Report: Topic Frequency Heatmap
Section 2 — Exam Strategy: How to Win the Clock
Section 3 — Unit 1 Deep Mastery: Data Science, NumPy, Pandas, Web Scraping
Section 4 — Unit 2 Deep Mastery: Data Wrangling (heaviest unit)
Section 5 — Unit 3 Deep Mastery: Matplotlib & Seaborn
Section 6 — Exam Tactics: MCQ Armory, Part B Playbook, Part C Masterclass
Unit I — NumPy & Pandas Foundations (32% of marks)
Unit II — Data Wrangling (22% of marks)
Unit III — Visualisation (18% of marks)
Unit IV — Web Scraping (16% of marks)
Unit V — Advanced Topics (12% of marks)
dropna / fillna — Critical topic (4/4 papers, 96% confidence)
NumPy zeros / ones / eye — Critical topic (4/4 papers, 96% confidence)
Series vs DataFrame — Critical topic (4/4 papers)
merge / concat / join — Critical topic (4/4 papers, 92% confidence, Part B+C)
Data Wrangling definition — Critical topic (4/4 papers)
Histogram / Box Plot — Critical topic (4/4 papers, 92% confidence, Part B+C)
Outlier Detection (IQR Method) — High priority (3/4 papers, 78% confidence)
pivot_table — High priority (3/4 papers)
Web Scraping / BeautifulSoup — High priority (3/4 papers, 75% confidence)
pairplot / seaborn — High priority (3/4 papers)
3D Surface Plot — Medium priority (2/4 papers, Part C favourite)
Standardization vs Normalization — Medium priority (2/4 papers)
MultiIndex — Low priority (1/4 papers, appeared once 2021 only, skip)
melt / stack — Low priority (1/4 papers, skip)
Topics Safe to Skip (negative ROI): MultiIndex, melt/stack, JSON/XML parsing, Hadoop/Spark, R syntax, Panel data/xarray
Data Science: extracting knowledge from data using scientific methods for decisions. NOT storing data.
Facets of Data: Structured (SQL/CSV), Unstructured (text/images), Semi-structured (JSON/XML)
NumPy: Numerical Python — vectorised array operations ~100x faster than Python loops
NumPy Array Creation: array(), zeros(), ones(), eye(), arange(), linspace(), reshape()
Pandas Series: 1D labelled array (single column). Memory: S=Single dimension.
Pandas DataFrame: 2D labelled data structure with named rows and columns. Memory: D=Double.
loc vs iloc: loc=label-based (inclusive end), iloc=integer position (exclusive end)
Data Wrangling Pipeline: Gather → Assess → Clean → Store (60-80% of data scientist's time)
Missing Data Types: MCAR (safe to drop), MAR (impute), MNAR (danger zone, investigate)
dropna(): axis=0=rows(default), axis=1=columns; how='any'(default) or 'all'; thresh=n
fillna(): fill with mean/median/mode/ffill/bfill or group-wise mean. Mean=symmetric, Median=skewed.
merge(): SQL-style join on column key. inner/outer/left/right. MCJ Rule: Merge=column key.
concat(): stack DataFrames. axis=0=vertical(rows), axis=1=horizontal(cols). Use ignore_index=True.
join(): index-based merge. Default=left join. Like merge with left_index=right_index=True.
pivot_table(): handles duplicates via aggfunc. pivot() raises ValueError on duplicates.
IQR Outlier Detection: Lower=Q1-1.5*IQR, Upper=Q3+1.5*IQR. Memory: FENCE mnemonic.
Z-score Outlier: |z|>3 is outlier. z=(x-mean)/std. Assumes normal distribution.
Standardization (Z-score scaling): z=(x-μ)/σ → mean=0, std=1. Use StandardScaler. For SVM/PCA/LR.
Normalization (Min-Max scaling): x'=(x-min)/(max-min) → [0,1]. Use MinMaxScaler. For KNN/NN.
Binning: pd.cut()=equal width, pd.qcut()=equal frequency. Converts continuous to categorical.
GroupBy: Split-Apply-Combine. agg()=summary stats, transform()=same shape, apply()=flexible.
Matplotlib: plt.figure()=canvas, plt.plot()=draw, plt.show()=display. Alias: import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
Histogram: plt.hist(data, bins=20). Default bins=10. Bars touch (continuous). Trap: parameter is 'bins' not 'buckets'.
Box Plot Anatomy: Q1=bottom, Q2=median line, Q3=top, IQR=box height, whiskers=1.5*IQR, dots=outliers
3D Surface Plot: Requires mpl_toolkits.mplot3d import + projection='3d'. Uses meshgrid + plot_surface(X,Y,Z).
Seaborn: high-level statistical plotting on top of Matplotlib. Accepts data=df and x='col_name' strings.
pairplot(): Seaborn function (NOT matplotlib). Grid of scatter plots + diagonal distribution plots.
savefig(): call BEFORE show(). show() clears buffer. Use bbox_inches='tight'. Format: 'png','pdf','svg'.
tight_layout(): prevents subplot overlap. Call BEFORE plt.show(). Order: create → tight_layout() → savefig() → show().
Web Scraping: requests=fetch HTML, BeautifulSoup=parse/extract. Two-library pattern always needed.
Memory Mnemonic: PCKD = Process→Collect→Knowledge→Decide (Data Science pipeline)
Memory Mnemonic: EZOC = Eye/Zeros/Ones/Concatenate (core NumPy functions)
Memory Mnemonic: S=Single (Series=1D), D=Double (DataFrame=2D)
Memory Mnemonic: MCJ = Merge(column key) / Concat(stack) / Join(index-based)
Memory Mnemonic: FENCE = Find quartiles→Establish IQR→Name bounds→Check each point→Eliminate outliers
Memory Mnemonic: STANDARD has STD in it → StandardScaler uses standard deviation
Memory Mnemonic: MCAR=safe, MAR=maybe, MNAR=must check (danger increases)
Memory Mnemonic: loc=LOCation by name (street address), iloc=Integer position (cinema seat)
Exam Trap: pd.read_csv() — NOT open_csv / load_csv / import_csv. Only read_csv exists.
Exam Trap: loc[0:2] includes row 2 (inclusive); iloc[0:2] does NOT include row 2 (exclusive).
Exam Trap: pivot() raises ValueError on duplicates; use pivot_table() for duplicate (index,col) pairs.
Exam Trap: pd.cut()=equal width bins; pd.qcut()=equal frequency/count bins.
Exam Trap: Standardization uses mean+std → unbounded output. Normalization uses min+max → [0,1]. NOT the same.
Exam Trap: pairplot() is SEABORN — NOT matplotlib. Most common MCQ trap for Unit 3.
Exam Trap: savefig() BEFORE show(). show() clears the buffer — saving after gives blank file.
Exam Trap: 3D plot needs BOTH mpl_toolkits.mplot3d import AND projection='3d' in add_subplot.
Exam Trap: dropna() axis=0=rows(default), axis=1=columns; how='any'=default vs 'all'.
Exam Trap: No .range() method in Pandas. Use max()-min() instead.
Exam Trap Pattern: Fake function names — pd.open_csv, df.sort, np.merge, df.top() NONE exist.
Golden Rule: Spend first 3 minutes reading ALL Part B/C questions and circling picks before writing.
Part B Safe Picks: dropna/fillna + merge/concat + pivot_table + outlier IQR (highest confidence 4 topics)
Part C Safe Combo: Visualisation question + Data Wrangling question (appeared in 3/4 papers).
Mistake #1: Answering all 5 Part B questions — 5th gets zero credit, wastes 18 minutes.
Mistake #2: Writing theory without code in Part B/C. Answer without code capped at 40% marks.
Mistake #3: Confusing merge/concat/join in MCQ. Merge=SQL on key, Concat=stack, Join=index.
First Principle: NumPy vectorisation — operations applied element-wise using compiled C code, no Python loops.
First Principle: fit_transform on train only; transform (not refit) on test data to prevent data leakage.
First Principle: This is a programming course — every Part B/C answer MUST contain code or it is capped at 40%.
Year Evolution Insight: Shift from theory-heavy (Nov 2021) to code-heavy (Nov 2024) papers.
Question Bank Unit 1 Short Answers: Define Data Science, NumPy vs Python lists, eye/zeros/ones, Series/DataFrame missing data
Question Bank Unit 1 Long Answers: NumPy 2D operations, CSV with Pandas, Web API to DataFrame, Web scraping with BS4
Question Bank Unit 2: Data Wrangling steps, pivoting, missing data strategies, standardization vs normalization, binning
Question Bank Unit 3: Static vs interactive viz, subplots, pairplot vs scatter, Seaborn vs Matplotlib, 3D surface plot

nodes.label: Site README — React + TypeScript + Vite template with ESLint configuration
Site Tech Stack: React + TypeScript + Vite + ESLint (vitejs/plugin-react with Oxc/SWC)
requests library: HTTP GET requests for web scraping and API calls. response.status_code 200=success.
BeautifulSoup (bs4): parse HTML. find()=first tag, find_all()=all tags, select()=CSS selector.
Large Dataset Strategies: CSS = Chunking(read_csv chunksize), Sampling(df.sample), Store-efficiently(Parquet/HDF5)
String Manipulation: .str accessor — .lower()/.upper()/.strip()/.replace()/.split()/.contains()/.extract()
Subplots: plt.subplots(rows, cols). axes[row,col] indexing. plt.suptitle() for overall title.
annotate() vs text(): annotate()=text+arrow pointer to data coord; text()=plain text at (x,y), no arrow.

hyperedges.label: Critical Topics Cluster (4/4 papers): dropna/fillna, NumPy array creation, Series vs DataFrame, merge/concat/join, histogram/boxplot, data wrangling definition
Part B Recommended 4 Picks: dropna/fillna + merge/concat + pivot_table + outlier IQR
MCJ Triad: merge / concat / join — most common confusion trap in exam
Scaling Techniques: Standardization (StandardScaler, z-score, mean=0/std=1) vs Normalization (MinMaxScaler, min-max, [0,1])
Web Scraping Stack: requests (fetch) + BeautifulSoup (parse) — two-library pattern always needed
